summary: Code-level static review of Lernio AI (static HTML/CSS/JS + 7 Vercel serverless functions). Ran npm install, npm run check, npm run build (all PASS). Linted /app/js, /app/api, /app/service-worker.js (no issues). Performed static review of all 12 checklist items per the request. 11/12 PASS, 1 minor stylistic deviation in mobile bottom nav count. No critical bugs found. No live API calls or browser tests were attempted (out of scope per user request).

static_review_checklist.notes: Checked 38 JS files and 14 mapped static assets. Exit 0.
dist/ contains index.html, manifest.json, service-worker.js, css/, js/, data/, assets/ (with notes/, mcqs/, logo.png, logo.jpeg).
ESLint reported no issues across all three targets.
grep found 0 occurrences of any forbidden token in css/auth.css.

index.html:362 registers '/service-worker.js' (absolute). index.html:20 references 'manifest.json' (relative). Both resolve correctly at root. Stylistic: manifest could use leading '/' for consistency, but functionally correct.

static_review_checklist.item: Mobile bottom nav has primary items + 'More' sheet
static_review_checklist.notes: #mobile-more-sheet exists at index.html:254 with .mobile-more-sheet styles at layout.css:42. However, the bottom nav has 4 primary items (Home, Notes, AI-FAB, Quiz) + 1 More button = 5 buttons total, not 5 primary + More (= 6) as the request implies. The HTML comment self-describes this as '5 primary items, no overflow' counting the More button as one of the 5. The 'More' sheet itself contains: Analytics, Settings, Login, Logout (4 secondary items).

static_review_checklist.item: js/ai.js exposes clearChat + retry-last + copy-message + 7 chips
static_review_checklist.notes: clearChat: line 213. _copyMessage: line 179 (+ copy button line 155). _retryLast: line 196 (+ retry button line 158). All 7 chips present in index.html:216-222 and ai.js:299-308: explain, exam, summary, mcqs, hinglish, weak, plan.

static_review_checklist.item: js/dashboard.js renders count-up tiles + Today's Goal
static_review_checklist.notes: data-count tiles at js/dashboard.js:101/106/111/116 (totalNotes, totalQuizzes, totalAttempts, avg%). Today's Goal eyebrow at line 125 (rendered at line 121). Animation routine queries .stat-tile-value[data-count] at line 363.

static_review_checklist.item: All 7 /api/* endpoints return graceful JSON when env vars missing
static_review_checklist.notes: chat.js: 503 JSON if N8N_CHAT_WEBHOOK_URL missing. ai-hint.js: 503 JSON if N8N_HINT_WEBHOOK_URL missing. ai.js: 503 JSON if GEMINI_API_KEY missing. progress.js: throws caught in try/catch -> 503 JSON 'Progress sync is temporarily unavailable...'. drive-notes.js + drive-file.js: errors routed through getDriveNotesErrorPayload(error) helper -> structured JSON. config.js: needs no env, returns static JSON. None of them crash.

12 production vars present: N8N_CHAT_WEBHOOK_URL, N8N_HINT_WEBHOOK_URL, GEMINI_API_KEY, GEMINI_MODEL, FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_KEY, FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID, FIREBASE_CLIENT_EMAIL, FIREBASE_PRIVATE_KEY, GOOGLE_DRIVE_FOLDER_ID, GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL, GOOGLE_PROJECT_ID, GOOGLE_PRIVATE_KEY. PORT is documented separately under 'Local dev only'.

static_review_checklist.item: No real secrets in repo (only env-var references)
static_review_checklist.notes: No private keys, no service-account JSON, no n8n webhook URLs, no Gemini key found anywhere. Only one hardcoded value: Firebase web apiKey 'AIzaSy...' at js/firebase-config.js:5 along with authDomain/projectId/appId. This is the PUBLIC Firebase Web SDK config which Google explicitly designs to be client-side; security is enforced via Firestore/Storage Rules + App Check. The file already contains an inline comment acknowledging this. Industry-standard, not a leak.

minor.issue: Mobile bottom nav has 4 primary destinations (Home/Notes/AI/Quiz) + More, totalling 5 buttons. Request stated '5 primary items + More sheet' (= 6). Off by one vs spec; functionally fine and matches HTML comment.

action_items: OPTIONAL: If spec strictly requires 5 primary destinations + More button, add a 5th primary item (e.g., Analytics) to .nav-mobile-inner in index.html and remove it from #mobile-more-sheet. Current 4+More layout is acceptable on phones <=375px.
OPTIONAL: Change index.html:20 from href="manifest.json" to href="/manifest.json" for absolute-path consistency with the service-worker registration.
OPTIONAL: Run `npm audit fix` to clear the 8 low + 1 moderate vulnerabilities (none are blocking).
critical_code_review_comments: Firebase web apiKey is hardcoded in /app/js/firebase-config.js:5. This is PUBLIC by Firebase design and the file documents it inline; safe by industry standard. Just be sure Firestore/Storage rules and App Check are enforced in the live project to prevent abuse.

/api/progress.js initAdmin() throws synchronously when no Firebase creds are present; the catch in handler() converts that to a 503. Works correctly, but consider returning a more specific error code (e.g., 501 Not Implemented or a structured 'configError' flag) so the frontend can distinguish 'misconfigured' from 'transient outage'.

/api/ai.js (Gemini fallback) is wired to read req.body directly without the robust readBody() helper that chat.js / ai-hint.js use. On Vercel this is fine because Vercel parses JSON automatically, but on the local Express server (local-server.js) this could mis-parse. Low priority.
/app/scripts/check-js.js is referenced as the project's own 'JS syntax + asset path validator' and passed cleanly; good.
service-worker.js was linted clean and is registered correctly.

success_rate.frontend: n/a (frontend testing explicitly out of scope)

test_credentials: Per /app/memory/test_credentials.md, no test users were seeded. None required for static review.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Next iteration: if user enables live API testing, set N8N_CHAT_WEBHOOK_URL / N8N_HINT_WEBHOOK_URL / GEMINI_API_KEY / FIREBASE_* / GOOGLE_* in /app/.env, run `npm run dev` (Express on :8080) and exercise /api/chat, /api/ai-hint, /api/progress, /api/drive-notes, /api/drive-file via curl. The local-server.js maps /api/* to the same handler files Vercel uses, so behavior should be identical. Browser/playwright testing was explicitly excluded by the user this iteration.
rca_of_the_issue: No bug being fixed in this iteration. This was a verification pass on E1's full production-grade upgrade. The only deviation found (mobile nav 4+More vs spec 5+More) is a minor count mismatch, not a functional defect.
